nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Core ORM models: Team, Player, Game, RankingHistory, Season, APIUsage, UpdateTask, Prediction, APPollRanking plus ConferenceType enum

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy engine/session configuration; init_db, get_db (FastAPI dependency), reset_db; supports SQLite and PostgreSQL via DATABASE_URL

nodes.name: position_service.py
nodes.summary: Position group strength calculation service; loads position weights config, scores each position group (QB, OL, RB, WR, TE, DL, LB, DB, ST) from Player recruiting ratings, produces ELO bonus

nodes.name: Team
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for a CFB team with ELO rating, conference type, recruiting/transfer portal metrics; relationships: home_games, away_games, ranking_history, players

nodes.name: Player
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for a recruit/transfer: position, stars, rating, ranking, recruiting_year; indexed on (team_id, position)

nodes.name: Game
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy model for a game: home/away teams, scores, quarter scores, week, season, game_type (regular/bowl/playoff/conference_championship), is_processed, rating_changes

nodes.name: RankingHistory
nodes.summary: Weekly ranking snapshot: team ELO, rank, wins, losses, SOS per week/season; unique index on (team_id, season, week)

nodes.name: Season
nodes.summary: Season metadata: year (unique), current_week, is_active; only one active at a time

nodes.name: APIUsage
nodes.summary: CFBD API call tracking: endpoint, timestamp, status_code, response_time_ms, month for quota monitoring

nodes.name: UpdateTask
nodes.summary: Weekly update task tracking: task_id, status (started/running/completed/failed), trigger_type, duration_seconds, result_json

nodes.name: Prediction
nodes.summary: Pre-game ELO prediction: game_id (unique FK), predicted_winner, predicted scores, win_probability, team ELOs at prediction time, was_correct evaluated post-game

nodes.name: APPollRanking
nodes.summary: AP Poll weekly rankings for comparison: season, week, poll_type, rank, team_id; unique on (season, week, team_id)

nodes.name: ConferenceType
nodes.summary: Enum: POWER_5='P5', GROUP_5='G5', FCS='FCS'; affects base ELO rating and cross-tier matchup multipliers

nodes.name: load_position_weights
nodes.summary: Loads position_weights.json config: enabled flag, per-position weights (must sum to 1.0), max_bonus, top_players_per_position

nodes.name: get_position_group_scores
nodes.summary: For each position group queries top-N players by rating for team_id, returns dict of position to score (0-100 normalized)

nodes.name: calculate_position_strength
nodes.summary: Computes overall position strength bonus (0-max_bonus ELO points) by weighted sum of position group scores; returns 0.0 if no player data

nodes.name: check_bowl_games.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: queries Game table for game_type='bowl', prints matchups/scores/bowl names with processed/unprocessed summary

nodes.name: check_conference_championships.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: queries Game table for game_type='conference_championship', prints matchups/scores with summary

nodes.name: check_game_details.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: shows full details for a single game by ID including scores, processing status, game_type, rating changes

nodes.name: check_playoff_games.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: queries Game table for game_type='playoff', prints rounds/matchups/scores with summary by playoff round

nodes.name: check_postseason_column.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: checks via SQLite PRAGMA whether postseason_name column exists on games table

nodes.name: check_prediction_accuracy.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: queries Prediction table for was_correct, prints overall accuracy and per-season breakdown

nodes.name: check_ranking_history.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: queries RankingHistory for available seasons, max week, entry count; shows top-5 teams

nodes.name: check_ranking_history_data.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: shows RankingHistory aggregated by season/week with team count; flags data quality issues

nodes.name: check_team_games.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: shows all games for a given team/season with schedule gap detection; reports processed/unprocessed counts

nodes.name: backfill_historical_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Backfills Prediction records using pre-game ELO ratings from RankingHistory week N-1; supports dry-run and rollback modes

nodes.name: check_games_by_season.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: counts games by week for each season, shows totals

nodes.name: check_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: counts predictions by season, evaluated vs unevaluated, samples first 5 predictions with correctness status

nodes.name: check_ranking_duplicates.py
nodes.summary: Finds duplicate team/season/week entries in ranking_history via GROUP BY + HAVING count>1

nodes.name: cleanup_ranking_duplicates.py
nodes.summary: Removes duplicate ranking_history entries keeping highest ID; dry-run by default, --execute to commit

nodes.name: diagnose_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic: checks current_week in Season, counts games by week for weeks 8-15 to debug prediction generation

nodes.name: fix_current_week.py
nodes.summary: Maintenance script: updates Season.current_week via --year and --week CLI args; validates week 0-19 range

nodes.name: archive_season.py
nodes.summary: Marks season as is_active=False; verifies final snapshot at week 20 exists; copies docs to archive; creates season notes

nodes.name: evaluate_rating_systems.py
nodes.summary: Backtests different ELO configurations against historical results using accuracy, Brier score, and log loss metrics

nodes.name: fix_unprocessed_games.py
nodes.summary: Scans for games with scores that are marked unprocessed, reprocesses them through ELO algorithm with safety checks

nodes.name: investigate_elo_imbalances.py
nodes.summary: Analyzes ELO rating distribution for anomalies: outlier teams, conference imbalances, expected vs actual rating ranges

nodes.name: validate_season.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive season data validation: verifies all games imported/processed, ELO consistency; generates markdown validation report

nodes.name: check_bowl_game_weeks.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that fetches postseason games from CFBD API and reports bowl game week numbers to debug import issues.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Fetches postseason games, categorizes them into bowl/playoff/conference-championship buckets, and prints week number analysis.

nodes.name: debug_championships.py
nodes.summary: Debug script that compares CFBD API championship game results with and without the classification=fbs filter to diagnose filtering issues.

nodes.name: debug_championships
nodes.summary: Runs two API queries for championship weeks (with and without FBS classification filter) and reports discrepancies.

nodes.name: diagnose_missing_games.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that checks game availability by team for specified weeks, flagging missing games that may be FCS opponents.

nodes.name: check_team_games
nodes.summary: Queries CFBD week-by-week for a specific team's games and prints completion status and summary.

nodes.name: import_real_data.py
nodes.summary: Core data import orchestrator that fetches teams, regular-season games, conference championships, bowl games, playoff games, and AP Poll rankings from CFBD and writes them to the database while processing ELO ratings.

nodes.name: import_teams
nodes.summary: Imports FBS teams with recruiting, talent, returning production, and transfer portal data; creates or updates team records and initializes ELO ratings.

nodes.name: import_conference_championships
nodes.summary: Fetches weeks 14-15 from CFBD, filters for conference championship games, creates or updates game records with quarter scores, and processes ELO.

nodes.name: import_bowl_games
nodes.summary: Fetches postseason games, filters out conference championships and CFP games, imports bowl games with quarter scores, and processes ELO.

nodes.name: import_playoff_games
nodes.summary: Fetches postseason games, identifies CFP playoff games by keyword, assigns week numbers by round, and imports with ELO processing.

nodes.name: import_ap_poll_rankings
nodes.summary: Fetches AP Poll data for a specific week and upserts APPollRanking records in the database.

nodes.name: parse_game_date
nodes.summary: Parses ISO 8601 startDate from CFBD API response, returning None for missing dates rather than a fallback timestamp.

nodes.name: validate_api_connection
nodes.summary: Tests CFBD API connectivity by fetching teams and reporting success or failure.

nodes.name: check_for_duplicates
nodes.summary: Queries the database for game records sharing the same home/away team, week, and season to detect duplicate imports.

nodes.name: validate_import_results
nodes.summary: Runs duplicate detection, verifies game counts, and prints an import validation summary report.

nodes.name: get_or_create_fcs_team
nodes.summary: Returns or creates an FCS team placeholder with is_fcs=True and a zeroed ELO rating.

nodes.name: backfill_quarter_scores.py
nodes.summary: Backfills missing quarter scores (q1–q4) for existing game records by fetching line scores from CFBD, with dry-run and batch commit support.

nodes.name: QuarterScoreBackfiller
nodes.summary: Encapsulates the backfill workflow: queries games with NULL quarter scores, fetches line scores from CFBD, validates and commits results, and reports statistics.

nodes.name: get_games_needing_backfill
nodes.summary: Queries Game records where q1_home IS NULL, optionally filtered by season and limited in count.

nodes.name: backfill_game
nodes.summary: Fetches line scores for a single game from CFBD, updates the Game model, validates quarter totals, and commits unless in dry-run mode.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full backfill loop with rate-limiting sleep, batch commits, and final summary.

nodes.name: check_week11_api.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that inspects CFBD Week 11 API data to debug current-week detection, checking both camelCase and snake_case score field names.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Calls get_current_week and get_games for week 11, categorizes games as completed or upcoming, and prints field-name inspection results.

nodes.name: debug_cfbd_response.py
nodes.summary: Minimal debug script that fetches week 10 games and pretty-prints the raw JSON response structure for the first three games.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Fetches week 10 regular-season games and dumps full JSON for the first three to stdout for API response inspection.

nodes.name: import_ap_poll_only.py
nodes.summary: Standalone script to import AP Poll rankings for specified weeks without touching game data or resetting the database.

nodes.name: import_ap_poll_for_week
nodes.summary: Fetches AP Poll data for a single week and upserts APPollRanking records, matching teams by name.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Interactive entry point that auto-detects season/week, accepts user input for range, and iterates import_ap_poll_for_week.

nodes.name: test_cfbd_data.py
nodes.summary: Quick smoke-test script that checks CFBD data availability for weeks 8-15 of the 2025 season.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loops over weeks 8-15, calls get_games for each, and reports game count or error.

nodes.name: update_games.py
nodes.summary: Incremental game import script that adds new games for specified weeks without resetting the database, creating FCS team placeholders as needed.

nodes.name: update_games
nodes.summary: Iterates over weeks, fetches games from CFBD, creates FCS placeholders, skips duplicates, fetches quarter scores for completed games, and writes new Game records.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Interactive entry point that auto-detects season/week, prompts for start/end weeks, and calls update_games.

nodes.name: weekly_update.py
nodes.summary: Weekly cron orchestrator that performs pre-flight checks (active season, current week detection, API quota) before running import_real_data.py in incremental mode.

nodes.name: is_active_season
nodes.summary: Returns True if today falls within the active CFB season window (August through configured season end date).

nodes.name: check_api_usage
nodes.summary: Returns True if monthly CFBD API usage is below 90% threshold, logging a critical message if exceeded.

nodes.name: get_current_week_wrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps CFBDClient.get_current_week with logging and exception handling for the weekly update pre-flight check.

nodes.name: validate_week_number
nodes.summary: Validates that a week number falls within 0-19 (covers preseason through CFP), returning False and logging errors for out-of-range values.

nodes.name: update_current_week
nodes.summary: Queries the max week among processed games and updates Season.current_week in the database as a redundancy measure.

nodes.name: run_import_script
nodes.summary: Executes import_real_data.py as a subprocess in incremental mode with a 30-minute timeout, returning the exit code.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that sequences three pre-flight checks then calls run_import_script and update_current_week on success.

nodes.name: cfbd_client.py
nodes.summary: CFBD API client providing methods to fetch teams, games, recruiting, transfer portal, AP Poll, and quarter-by-quarter line scores, with automatic usage tracking and quota monitoring.

nodes.name: CFBDClient
nodes.summary: HTTP client for CollegeFootballData.com API covering teams, games, recruiting rankings, player data, transfer portal, AP Poll, and line scores; uses Bearer token auth and @track_api_usage decorator.

nodes.name: track_api_usage
nodes.summary: Decorator that logs every API call to the api_usage table with endpoint, timestamp, status code, and response time, then checks warning thresholds.

nodes.name: get_monthly_usage
nodes.summary: Returns a dict of API call count, monthly limit, percentage used, remaining calls, average per day, and top endpoints for a given month.

nodes.name: check_usage_warnings
nodes.summary: Logs critical/warning messages at 80/90/95% thresholds, suppressing duplicate warnings per month.

nodes.name: get_season_end_date
nodes.summary: Reads CFB_SEASON_END_DATE env var (default Feb 1) and returns (month, day) tuple for season boundary calculations.

nodes.name: get_current_season
nodes.summary: Determines the current CFB season year based on calendar date and the configured season end date, accounting for the January playoff period.

nodes.name: get_current_week
nodes.summary: Fetches all regular-season games and returns the highest week number that has completed (non-null, non-0-0) scores.

nodes.name: estimate_current_week
nodes.summary: Fallback calendar-based week estimator using weeks elapsed since the first Saturday after Labor Day.

nodes.name: get_teams
nodes.summary: Fetches all FBS teams for a given season year from the /teams/fbs endpoint.

nodes.name: get_games
nodes.summary: Fetches games from the /games endpoint with optional week, team, season type, and classification filters.

nodes.name: get_recruiting_rankings
nodes.summary: Fetches team-level 247Sports composite recruiting rankings for a class year from /recruiting/teams.

nodes.name: get_recruiting_players
nodes.summary: Fetches individual player recruiting data (stars, rating, ranking, position) from /recruiting/players, returning an empty list on API failure.

nodes.name: get_team_talent
nodes.summary: Fetches team talent composite scores from the /talent endpoint.

nodes.name: get_returning_production
nodes.summary: Fetches returning production percentages (percentPPA) from the /player/returning endpoint.

nodes.name: get_transfer_portal
nodes.summary: Fetches transfer portal entries from the /player/portal endpoint.

nodes.name: get_ap_poll
nodes.summary: Fetches rankings from /rankings, filters to AP Top 25 poll entries, and returns a flat list of team ranking dicts.

nodes.name: get_game_line_scores
nodes.summary: Fetches quarter-by-quarter line scores from /games/teams, matches the specific game by team names, and returns {home: [q1,q2,q3,q4], away: [q1,q2,q3,q4]} or None.

nodes.name: compare_rankings.py
nodes.summary: Utility that compares ELO rankings against the AP Poll, analyzes prediction accuracy, and displays ELO insights such as biggest rating changes and strength of schedule leaders.

nodes.name: compare_with_ap_poll
nodes.summary: Fetches ELO rankings and compares them to a hardcoded AP Poll reference, computing exact matches and average positional difference.

nodes.name: analyze_prediction_accuracy
nodes.summary: Queries all processed games and measures how often ELO correctly predicted the winner, listing biggest upsets.

nodes.name: show_elo_insights
nodes.summary: Displays biggest ELO rating gainers/decliners and strength-of-schedule leaders from current rankings.

nodes.name: import_player_data.py
nodes.summary: Script to import individual player recruiting data from CFBD for all FBS teams or a single team, with API quota checks, dry-run mode, and upsert logic.

nodes.name: check_api_quota
nodes.summary: Estimates API calls needed (~133 for full FBS import) and warns if projected usage would exceed 90% of the monthly quota.

nodes.name: import_players_for_team
nodes.summary: Fetches player recruiting data for one team and upserts Player records in the database, committing in batches of 100.

nodes.name: import_all_teams
nodes.summary: Iterates over all FBS teams in the database and calls import_players_for_team for each, with rate-limit sleep between teams.

nodes.name: verify_import
nodes.summary: Queries total player count and teams with players for a given recruiting year, then prints the top 5 recruits by ranking.

nodes.name: backfill_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Backfills prediction records for completed games that lack predictions, enabling historical comparison features (EPIC-010).

nodes.name: backfill_predictions
nodes.summary: Iterates all completed games for a season and calls create_and_store_prediction for any game missing a prediction record.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that opens a DB session and runs backfill_predictions for the 2025 season.

nodes.name: generate_all_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Generates and persists predictions for all upcoming games (not just next week), supporting playoff and special-case scenarios.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI args, resolves season via CFBDClient if not provided, calls generate_predictions with next_week=False, and saves all predictions to the DB.

nodes.name: generate_predictions.py
nodes.summary: Generates and persists predictions for the upcoming week's games after deployment.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Calls generate_predictions for next-week games and saves resulting Prediction records to the database.

nodes.name: generate_ranking_comparison_report.py
nodes.summary: Generates a before/after markdown report comparing rankings pre- and post-implementation of the quarter-weighted ELO algorithm.

nodes.name: RankingComparisonReport
nodes.summary: Orchestrates capturing current rankings, recalculating from scratch with the new algorithm, and producing a markdown comparison report with top-25 changes and biggest movers.

nodes.name: capture_current_rankings
nodes.summary: Queries all teams ordered by ELO rating and returns a ranked list of (rank, name, rating) tuples.

nodes.name: recalculate_rankings_from_scratch
nodes.summary: Resets all team ratings to preseason values then reprocesses every game in chronological order via RankingService.

nodes.name: generate_comparison_report
nodes.summary: Writes a markdown report with top-25 rank changes, biggest movers, algorithm impact analysis, and garbage-time detection examples.

nodes.name: start_new_season.py
nodes.summary: Season initialization script that archives the previous season, creates a new Season record, resets team ELO ratings to preseason values, and saves preseason rankings (EPIC-024).

nodes.name: validate_new_season
nodes.summary: Validates that the target season does not already exist in the database before initialization.

nodes.name: archive_previous_season
nodes.summary: Sets the previous season's is_active flag to False to archive it.

nodes.name: create_new_season
nodes.summary: Creates a new Season record with week=1 and is_active=True.

nodes.name: reset_team_ratings
nodes.summary: Calls RankingService.calculate_preseason_rating for every team and persists the results as elo_rating and initial_rating.

nodes.name: save_preseason_rankings
nodes.summary: Writes week=0 RankingHistory entries for all teams as preseason ranking snapshots.

nodes.name: validate_season_data
nodes.summary: Post-initialization validation checking season existence, absence of existing games, and presence of preseason rankings.

nodes.name: ranking_service.py
nodes.summary: Core domain logic implementing the Modified ELO ranking algorithm with preseason factors, progressive K-factor, garbage time detection, SOS calculation, prediction generation, and accuracy tracking.

nodes.name: RankingService
nodes.summary: Main service class providing Modified ELO ranking with preseason initialization, progressive K-factor (64→48→32), quarter-weighted margin-of-victory with garbage time detection, conference multipliers, SOS calculation, and weekly ranking snapshots.

nodes.name: get_k_factor
nodes.summary: Returns the progressive K-factor for a given week: 64 for weeks 1-4, 48 for weeks 5-8, and 32 for week 9+ (EPIC-027).

nodes.name: _calculate_preseason_bonuses
nodes.summary: Computes individual preseason bonus components (base, recruiting, transfer portal, returning production, position strength) for use in rating initialization and the preseason simulator endpoint.

nodes.name: calculate_preseason_rating
nodes.summary: Calculates a team's preseason ELO rating by summing bonus components and optionally blending with previous season ELO using configurable regression (EPIC-030).

nodes.name: _calculate_position_strength_bonus
nodes.summary: Feature-flagged calculation of a position group strength bonus from player recruiting data; gracefully returns 0.0 when disabled or on error.

nodes.name: initialize_team_rating
nodes.summary: Sets a team's elo_rating and initial_rating to the calculated preseason rating and commits to DB.

nodes.name: _get_previous_season_elo
nodes.summary: Retrieves a team's final ELO from the previous season via ranking_history, preferring the week=999 postseason sentinel over the last regular-season entry.

nodes.name: save_final_season_snapshot
nodes.summary: Writes idempotent week=999 sentinel entries to ranking_history capturing each team's true postseason-final ELO before the next preseason reset (EPIC-030).

nodes.name: get_preseason_components
nodes.summary: Returns raw preseason bonus component data for all FBS teams, used by the preseason simulator endpoint for client-side recalculation (EPIC-032).

nodes.name: calculate_expected_score
nodes.summary: Computes the standard ELO expected win probability for team A against team B using the logistic formula.

nodes.name: calculate_mov_multiplier
nodes.summary: Calculates a logarithmic margin-of-victory multiplier for ELO adjustments, capped at MAX_MOV_MULTIPLIER (2.5).

nodes.name: calculate_quarter_weighted_mov
nodes.summary: Calculates quarter-by-quarter MOV multiplier, applying 0.25 weight to Q4 scoring when game enters garbage time (differential > 21 after Q3) per EPIC-021.

nodes.name: get_conference_multiplier
nodes.summary: Returns winner/loser ELO change multipliers based on conference tier matchup (FBS vs FCS, P5 vs G5, same tier).

nodes.name: process_game
nodes.summary: Validates and processes a completed game: applies home field advantage, computes ELO changes using progressive K-factor and MOV multiplier, updates team ratings and records, evaluates prediction accuracy, and commits to DB.

nodes.name: get_season_record
nodes.summary: Queries the games table for season-specific win/loss counts for a team, replacing cumulative team-table records (EPIC-024).

nodes.name: calculate_sos
nodes.summary: Calculates strength of schedule as the average ELO rating of all opponents faced in processed, rankings-eligible games for a season.

nodes.name: get_current_rankings
nodes.summary: Returns season-specific rankings from ranking_history for the current week, enabling accurate historical season browsing (EPIC-024).

nodes.name: save_weekly_rankings
nodes.summary: Persists current team ELO ratings, season records, and SOS values to ranking_history for a given week, with idempotent upsert behavior.

nodes.name: reset_season
nodes.summary: Resets all team ELO ratings to recalculated preseason values and zeroes win/loss records for a fresh season start.

nodes.name: generate_predictions
nodes.summary: Standalone function that queries unprocessed games (optionally filtered by week/team/season) and returns prediction dictionaries with win probabilities and estimated scores.

nodes.name: _calculate_game_prediction
nodes.summary: Calculates win probabilities and predicted scores for a single game using ELO ratings and home field advantage.

nodes.name: _validate_prediction_teams
nodes.summary: Validates that both home and away teams exist and have positive ELO ratings before generating a prediction.

nodes.name: create_and_store_prediction
nodes.summary: Creates and persists a Prediction record for a future game using current ELO ratings, with duplicate guard (EPIC-009).

nodes.name: evaluate_prediction_accuracy
nodes.summary: Updates the was_correct flag on an existing Prediction record by comparing the predicted winner to the actual game winner (EPIC-009).

nodes.name: get_overall_prediction_accuracy
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate prediction accuracy statistics (total, evaluated, correct, percentage) optionally filtered by season (EPIC-009).

nodes.name: get_team_prediction_accuracy
nodes.summary: Computes per-team prediction accuracy statistics including breakdowns as favorite vs underdog (EPIC-009).

nodes.name: validate_week
nodes.summary: Validates that a week number falls within the acceptable range (0-15) for prediction generation.

nodes.name: validate_team_for_prediction
nodes.summary: Validates that a team exists and has a positive ELO rating suitable for prediction.

nodes.name: validate_predicted_score
nodes.summary: Clamps a predicted score to the valid range [0, 150].

nodes.name: validate_game_for_prediction
nodes.summary: Validates a game is eligible for prediction (unprocessed, valid week range).

nodes.name: reprocess_season.py
nodes.summary: Utility that resets and fully reprocesses all ranked games for a season to correct ELO imbalances, with dry-run support and before/after verification.

nodes.name: check_season_exists
nodes.summary: Verifies the target season record exists in the database before proceeding.

nodes.name: analyze_current_state
nodes.summary: Reports total, processed, and excluded game counts and detects ELO imbalances (home+away rating changes not summing to zero).

nodes.name: reset_season_data
nodes.summary: Resets all non-excluded games to unprocessed state and restores team ratings to initial_rating with zeroed records.

nodes.name: reprocess_season
nodes.summary: Instantiates RankingService and calls process_game on all unprocessed games in chronological order.

nodes.name: verify_reprocessing
nodes.summary: Confirms all games were processed and no ELO imbalances remain after reprocessing.

nodes.name: save_final_season_snapshot.py
nodes.summary: Utility that captures postseason-final ELO ratings into ranking_history as week=999 sentinel entries before the next preseason reset (EPIC-030).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that previews the top 10 teams by ELO, then calls RankingService.save_final_season_snapshot to persist the week=999 snapshot.

nodes.name: seed_data.py
nodes.summary: Seeds the database with sample teams (P5, G5, FCS) and two weeks of games for the 2024 season, initializing ratings and processing games via RankingService.

nodes.name: seed_database
nodes.summary: Resets the DB, creates 33 sample teams across conferences, processes two weeks of games through RankingService, and saves ranking history for weeks 1-2.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point exposing the full REST API for the Modified ELO college football ranking system, with endpoints for teams, games, predictions, rankings, seasons, stats, and admin operations including live CFBD data import.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI decorators for routing; admin endpoints are protected by an X-Admin-Key header matched against ADMIN_SECRET env var; background tasks are managed via FastAPI BackgroundTasks.

nodes.name: startup_event
nodes.summary: FastAPI startup event handler that initializes the database by calling init_db() to create all tables on application launch.

nodes.name: get_team
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single team by ID with season-specific ELO rating, wins/losses, rank, and SOS pulled from the active season's ranking history via RankingService.

nodes.name: update_team
nodes.summary: Updates team fields and automatically recalculates the preseason ELO rating via RankingService when any recruiting, transfer portal, or returning production field changes.

nodes.name: get_team_schedule
nodes.summary: Returns a team's full season schedule ordered by week/date, enriched with opponent details, scores, FCS flags, and postseason game metadata.

nodes.name: get_team_players
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, filtered list of players for a team sorted by recruiting rating, supporting optional filters for recruiting year and position.

nodes.name: get_team_position_strength
nodes.summary: Calculates position group strength scores and overall position bonus for a team using player recruiting data and weights from position_weights.json.

nodes.name: get_team_games
nodes.summary: Returns a team's processed game log enriched with ELO before/after deltas for each game, used for team game-log display.

nodes.name: get_matchup
nodes.summary: Head-to-head comparison endpoint returning both teams' ELO histories, historical matchup records, and win probabilities for neutral, home-A, and home-B scenarios.

nodes.name: get_games
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, multi-filter list of games sorted by week/date descending, supporting filters for season, week, team, and processing status.

nodes.name: create_game
nodes.summary: Creates a game record and immediately processes it through RankingService.process_game to update both teams' ELO ratings.

nodes.name: get_predictions
nodes.summary: Returns stored predictions for unprocessed games when available, falling back to on-the-fly generation via generate_predictions; supports week, team, and season filters.

nodes.name: get_historical_predictions
nodes.summary: Simulates predictions for a completed historical week by looking up teams' ELO ratings from ranking_history at game time and comparing against actual results for backtesting.

nodes.name: get_stored_predictions
nodes.summary: Returns stored prediction records enriched with game details and evaluation status, supporting filters for season, week, team, and evaluated-only.

nodes.name: get_prediction_comparison
nodes.summary: Compares ELO vs AP Poll prediction accuracy for a season by delegating to ap_poll_service.calculate_comparison_stats, returning a graceful empty state on failure.

nodes.name: get_rankings

nodes.name: story-cfbd-01-investigate-api-schema.md

Estimated 2-4 hours effort, high priority. Integration points: CFBDClient in src/integrations/cfbd_client.py and tests/unit/test_cfbd_client.py.

nodes.name: story-cfbd-02-fix-test-mocks.md
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for STORY-CFBD-02 (blocked by STORY-CFBD-01): fix 6 failing tests in test_cfbd_client.py where test mocks use incorrect field names. Failing tests include test_get_current_week_with_completed_games, test_get_current_week_week_1_only, test_get_current_week_ignores_missing_week_field, and 3 others. Goal: align test mocks with real CFBD API schema to restore CI/CD to green. Estimated 2-4 hours effort.

nodes.name: deploy-epic-004.sh

Includes colored terminal output for step visibility.

nodes.name: deploy-epic-009.sh

nodes.name: deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Main deployment script (requires root). Pulls latest code from git origin/main, installs requirements-prod.txt into the venv, restarts the cfb-rankings systemd service, and reloads nginx (with config test). App lives at /var/www/cfb-rankings. Database migration step is commented out for regular deployments.

nodes.name: fix-permissions.sh
nodes.summary: File ownership and permission fix script for EPIC-004 deployment. Sets www-data:www-data ownership recursively on /var/www/cfb-rankings, database file to 664, backup files, and .env file to 640. Intended to be run after initial deploy or when permission errors occur.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: One-time VPS initial setup script (requires root). Installs system packages (python3, nginx, certbot, git), creates /var/www/cfb-rankings, clones or accepts manually placed app files, creates Python venv, installs requirements-prod.txt, creates log directories, copies .env.example to .env and opens nano for editing, initializes the SQLite database, optionally runs import_real_data.py, sets www-data ownership, installs deploy/cfb-rankings.service to systemd, configures nginx from deploy/nginx.conf (substituting domain), and obtains a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate via certbot.

nodes.name: cfb-rankings.service
nodes.summary: Systemd service unit for the main FastAPI application. Runs as www-data, WorkingDirectory /var/www/cfb-rankings, ExecStart uses gunicorn with gunicorn_config.py and main:app. Type=simple, Restart=on-failure with 10s RestartSec. Sets PATH to venv/bin, DATABASE_URL, and CFBD_API_KEY via inline Environment directives (note: key is hardcoded in this file). WantedBy multi-user.target.

nodes.name: cfb-weekly-update.service
nodes.summary: Systemd oneshot service unit for the weekly data import job. Runs as www-data, loads env from /var/www/cfb-rankings/.env, executes scripts/weekly_update.py via venv Python. Logs append to /var/log/cfb-rankings/weekly-update.log. 30-minute timeout, Restart=no (timer handles scheduling). Security-hardened: NoNewPrivileges, PrivateTmp, ProtectSystem=strict, ProtectHome, ReadWritePaths limited to app and log dirs.

nodes.name: cfb-weekly-update.timer
nodes.summary: Systemd timer that triggers cfb-weekly-update.service every Sunday at 22:00 UTC (5:00 PM Eastern during EST, 6:00 PM Eastern during EDT). Persistent=true ensures missed runs execute on next boot. RandomizedDelaySec=300 adds up to 5 minutes of jitter for network stability. WantedBy timers.target.

nodes.name: nginx.conf
nodes.summary: Nginx site configuration for college football rankings. HTTP (port 80) redirects to HTTPS. HTTPS (port 443) with TLS 1.2/1.3 via Let's Encrypt certificates: serves frontend static files from /var/www/cfb-rankings/frontend with 1-year cache headers for assets, and reverse-proxies /api/, /docs, /redoc, /openapi.json to Gunicorn at 127.0.0.1:8000 with 60s timeouts. Security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection.

nodes.name: 28.1.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.1 (Complete). Create CONTRIBUTING.md and DEVELOPMENT.md developer onboarding documentation covering local setup, environment configuration, test running, and contribution workflow. Zero-risk documentation-only change with no code modifications.

nodes.name: 28.2.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.2 (Complete). Create CODING-STANDARDS.md documentation, add pyproject.toml with Black and isort configuration (line-length=88, profile=black), create .flake8 config, and add requirements-dev.txt with development tooling. Establishes code quality toolchain configuration.

nodes.name: 28.3.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.3 (Complete). Add comprehensive Google-style docstrings to all core modules: main.py (20+ endpoints), ranking_service.py (15+ methods), models.py (10+ classes), schemas.py (8+ schemas), database.py (3+ functions), cfbd_client.py (8+ methods). Enables auto-generated OpenAPI documentation and IDE support.

nodes.name: 28.4.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.4 (Complete). Run isort on all Python files to standardize import statement ordering: 54 root-level .py files, 20 files in scripts/, and the tests/ directory. Uses isort with profile=black configuration for compatibility with Black formatter.

nodes.name: 28.5.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.5 (Complete). Reorganize project files by moving migrate_add_*.py scripts to migrations/ directory, and moving check_*.py, debug_*.py, diagnose_*.py scripts to diagnostics/ directory using git mv to preserve history. Improves project structure and separates concerns.

nodes.name: 28.6.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.6 (Complete, Medium-High risk). Create src/ package structure with api/, core/, models/, integrations/ subdirectories and move core files via git mv: main.py → src/api/, ranking_service.py → src/core/, models.py+schemas.py+database.py → src/models/, cfbd_client.py → src/integrations/. Update all import statements across the codebase to use src. prefix. Most significant refactor in the epic.

nodes.name: 28.7.story.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.7 (Complete). Archive one-off fix_*.py scripts to archive/ directory, move remaining utility scripts to utilities/, apply Black formatter to all Python files, and generate Flake8 lint report documenting remaining violations. Completes the codebase organization and formatting epic.

nodes.name: EPIC-003-DEV-SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-003 development summary for FCS game display feature. Documents completed implementation: FCS games stored with excluded_from_rankings=True flag, displayed on team schedules with gray/muted visual styling, opponent shown as team name or 'FCS Opponent'. Covers all 3 stories (model flag, API import, frontend display) and confirms zero regressions on existing FBS game rankings.

nodes.name: epic-003-fcs-game-display.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-003 definition document for FCS game display. Defines the problem (FCS games excluded from rankings but not visible to users), solution (store with excluded_from_rankings=True, display with visual distinction), and 3 stories: add Game model exclusion flag, import FCS games via CFBD API, and display FCS games on team schedules with grayed-out styling.

nodes.name: preseason-1.1-player-model.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.1 (Completed). Create Player SQLAlchemy model in src/models/models.py with fields: cfbd_athlete_id, name, team_id (FK), position, stars, rating, ranking, recruiting_year, created_at. Generate and run Alembic migration to add players table. Foundation for position strength preseason calculations.

nodes.name: preseason-1.2-cfbd-player-api.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.2 (Completed). Add get_recruiting_players() method to CFBDClient in src/integrations/cfbd_client.py, calling the CFBD /recruiting/players endpoint with year and team parameters. Returns list of player dicts with star ratings and position data for importing into the Player model.

nodes.name: preseason-1.3-position-service.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.3 (Completed). Create src/core/position_service.py with calculate_position_strength() function and position_weights.json config file. Weights: QB=0.30, OL=0.25, DL=0.20, DB=0.15, LB=0.05, RB=0.025, WR=0.025, TE=0.0, ST=0.0. Position scores normalized 0-100, bonus capped at max_bonus. Feature flag enabled=false by default.

nodes.name: preseason-1.4-player-import.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.4 (Completed). Create utilities/import_player_data.py script with CLI flags --year, --team, --dry-run, --force. Fetches recruiting player data from CFBD API and inserts into Player table, respecting existing records (skip or overwrite with --force). Supports importing one team or all FBS teams for a given year.

nodes.name: preseason-1.5-api-endpoints.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.5 (Completed). Add two FastAPI endpoints to src/api/main.py: GET /api/teams/{team_id}/players (filtering by recruiting_year, position, pagination via skip/limit, sorted by rating desc) and GET /api/teams/{team_id}/position-strength (returns enabled flag, position_scores, position_bonus, max_bonus, weights, recruiting_year). 18 integration tests created.

nodes.name: preseason-1.6-integration.md
nodes.summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.6 (Completed). Integrate position strength bonus into calculate_preseason_rating() in src/core/ranking_service.py via new _calculate_position_strength_bonus() helper. Feature flag (config['enabled']) gates the bonus; graceful degradation returns 0.0 on disabled/error/missing data. 10 unit tests cover all scenarios. Formula: base + recruiting + transfer + returning + position_strength_bonus.

nodes.name: story-001-games-page-display.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-001 Story 1 (Complete). Update games.html to display all season games including future/scheduled games. Fetches from /api/games endpoint and renders both completed (with scores) and upcoming (with date/time, TBD score) games. Covers full season game list view.

nodes.name: story-002-team-detail-schedule.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-001 Story 2 (Complete). Update team detail page to show full season schedule including future games. Team schedule section fetches from /api/teams/{id}/schedule and renders upcoming games with opponent, date, and predicted outcome alongside completed games with results.

nodes.name: story-003-visual-enhancements.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-001 Story 3 (Complete). Add visual styling to distinguish completed vs scheduled games across the app: completed games show W/L outcome badges with green/red styling, scheduled games show a clock icon and muted styling. CSS additions to frontend styles.

nodes.name: story-004-dynamic-season-detection-utility.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-002 Story 4 (Complete). Create utility functions to auto-detect the current season year and current week from the CFBD API, replacing hardcoded values in import scripts. Implements get_current_season() and get_current_week() in cfbd_client.py using CFBD /games endpoint with status=completed parameter.

nodes.name: story-005-remove-hardcoded-seasons.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-002 Story 5 (Complete). Remove all hardcoded year values (e.g., 2024) from import scripts and replace with dynamic detection using get_current_season(). Affects weekly_update.py, import scripts, and admin trigger endpoints. Eliminates manual year updates between seasons.

nodes.name: story-006-import-validation-completeness.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-002 Story 6 (Complete). Add import validation to check completeness after each weekly import: compare expected game count from CFBD API vs actual imported count, log warnings for missing games, and include completeness report in import output. Helps detect partial import failures.

nodes.name: story-007-game-model-exclusion-flag.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-003 Story 7 (Complete). Add excluded_from_rankings Boolean field (default False) to the Game SQLAlchemy model in src/models/models.py and create corresponding Alembic migration. Enables marking FCS games and other non-ranking games without deleting them from the database.

nodes.name: story-008-fcs-game-import-api.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-003 Story 8 (Complete). Update game import logic in cfbd_client.py to fetch FCS opponent games (where opponent is not an FBS team) and set excluded_from_rankings=True. Update /api/teams/{id}/schedule endpoint to include FCS games in response with is_fcs_opponent flag.

nodes.name: story-009-fcs-frontend-display.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-003 Story 9 (Complete). Display FCS games on team schedule pages with visual distinction: grayed-out/muted styling, 'FCS' label badge, opponent shown as team name or 'FCS Opponent'. Uses CSS .fcs-game class for styling, frontend-only change to team detail JavaScript rendering.

nodes.name: story-ap-comparison-data-fix.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for AP comparison page showing zero values. Root cause: AP poll data was not being imported during weekly updates. Fix imports AP poll rankings from CFBD /rankings endpoint alongside game data, stores in APPoll table, and comparison page correctly displays ELO vs AP Poll accuracy charts.

nodes.name: story-cfbd-01-investigate-api-schema.md
nodes.summary: Investigation story to examine the CFBD API response schema and document actual field naming conventions. Found that CFBD uses camelCase in JSON responses (e.g., homePoints, awayPoints, startDate) rather than snake_case assumed in early code. Produces schema reference document for use in cfbd_client.py and test mock updates.

nodes.name: story-cfbd-02-fix-test-mocks.md
nodes.summary: Fix story to update test mocks in the test suite to match actual CFBD API camelCase response schema (homePoints, awayPoints, startDate, etc.) discovered in story-cfbd-01. Corrects test mock data so unit tests accurately reflect real API responses and prevent false-passing tests.

nodes.name: story-fix-admin-endpoint-imports.md
nodes.summary: Fix story (Complete). Resolve ModuleNotFoundError in admin endpoints by correcting 3 import paths in src/api/main.py from bare 'from cfbd_client import ...' to 'from src.integrations.cfbd_client import ...'. Part of post-EPIC-028 src/ reorganization cleanup. Fixes 5 integration tests that were failing due to import errors.

nodes.name: story-fix-admin-updatetask-import.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for UnboundLocalError in trigger_update admin endpoint caused by UpdateTask class not being imported before use in exception handler. Fix imports UpdateTask at the top of the function or module scope. Resolves 4 failing unit tests in the admin endpoint test suite.

nodes.name: story-fix-cfbd-current-week-detection.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for CFBDClient.get_current_week() returning None instead of the correct week number. Root cause: query logic did not handle the case where all weeks had completed games, returning None instead of the max week. Fix adds fallback to return max completed week when no in-progress week is found. Resolves 2 failing unit tests.

nodes.name: story-fix-comparison-page-error.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for comparison page returning HTTP 500 when AP Poll data is unavailable. Replace hard failure with graceful error handling: return empty datasets with an informative message rather than crashing. Frontend displays 'AP Poll data not available' state instead of blank/broken chart.

nodes.name: story-fix-game-import-tests.md
nodes.summary: Fix story (Complete, ~30min). Resolve game import test assertion failures caused by mismatched expected vs actual field values in test fixtures after CFBD schema corrections. Updates test assertions and mock data to match corrected camelCase-to-snake_case field mappings in cfbd_client.py.

nodes.name: story-fix-integration-test-imports.md
nodes.summary: Fix story (Closed, not reproducible). Investigation into integration test import failures that could not be reproduced in a clean environment. Documents the investigation steps taken and confirms the issue was transient or environment-specific. No code changes made.

nodes.name: story-fix-rankings-teams-test-data.md
nodes.summary: Fix story (Complete). Update rankings and teams API test fixtures to create RankingHistory records instead of only Team objects, reflecting the post-EPIC-024 architecture change where rankings are stored in RankingHistory table rather than on Team directly. Also creates proper Game records in test setup. Fixes 8 failing integration tests.

nodes.name: story-fix-season-year-detection.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for CFBDClient.get_current_season() incorrectly advancing to the next season year during January playoff period. Fix adds date-range logic: if current date is in January and playoff games are still in progress, stay on the prior year's season rather than jumping to the new year. Ensures correct data imports during bowl/playoff season.

nodes.name: story-fix-testclient-fixture.md
nodes.summary: Fix story (Complete, ~1hr). Resolve TestClient fixture initialization error in integration tests caused by FastAPI app startup event failing during test setup. Fix configures test database override and ensures lifespan context is properly mocked or bypassed in test fixtures.

nodes.name: story-fix-weekly-update-exit-calls.md
nodes.summary: Fix story for weekly_update.py calling sys.exit() multiple times per error scenario (once in the error handler and again in the finally block), causing confusing exit codes. Refactor to call sys.exit() exactly once per error path, typically at the end of the main function. Fixes 3 failing unit tests.

nodes.name: story-game-dates-01-ui-display.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 1. Add game date column to games.html table, displaying formatted date (e.g., 'Jan 1') for each game. Frontend-only change using existing game_date field already returned by /api/games endpoint. Especially useful for bowl and playoff games that span multiple days in the same week.

nodes.name: story-game-dates-02-api-sorting.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 2. Update /api/games and /api/teams/{id}/schedule endpoints to sort results by week first, then by game_date as a secondary sort (null dates sorted last). SQLAlchemy query change using ORDER BY week ASC, game_date ASC NULLS LAST for consistent chronological ordering.

nodes.name: story-game-dates-03-consistency.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 3. Ensure consistent date display format across all views (games list, team schedule, predictions). Add relative date display for upcoming games (e.g., 'in 3 days', 'Tomorrow') to team schedule and prediction pages. Standardizes date presentation throughout the frontend.

nodes.name: story-import-playoff-predictions.md
nodes.summary: Story to import completed playoff game results and generate ELO-based predictions for the next playoff round. After each playoff round completes, runs import to update ELO ratings, then generates predictions for upcoming matchups. Supports the 12-team CFP bracket with first-round through championship predictions.

nodes.name: story-multi-year-comparison.md
nodes.summary: Story to add multi-year season selector to the AP Poll comparison page, allowing users to view ELO vs AP Poll accuracy for any historical season (not just the current one). Adds year dropdown to comparison.html, updates /api/comparison endpoint to accept optional year query parameter, defaults to current season.

nodes.name: story-playoff-01-diagnose-import-issues.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 1. Diagnose why 2024 CFP playoff games have 0-0 placeholder scores or are missing entirely. Investigation reveals: week validation capped at 15 (excluded weeks 16-19), API call limit of 1,000 was hit before playoff games, and season_type='postseason' not used for first-round games. Documents root causes for stories 2 and 3.

nodes.name: story-playoff-02-import-fix-2024-games.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 2. Import all 8 2024 CFP playoff games (4 first-round + 4 quarterfinals) with correct scores, dates, and ELO updates. Extends week validation from 0-15 to 0-19, increases API limit to 30,000, and handles TBD opponent placeholders for future rounds. One-time data fix for 2024 season.

nodes.name: story-playoff-03-sustainable-playoff-handling.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 3. Enhance cfbd_client.py and weekly_update.py to automatically handle postseason games in future seasons: detect weeks 16-19 as postseason, use season_type='postseason' for API calls, skip TBD-opponent games until results are available. Ensures playoff imports work without manual intervention in future years.

nodes.name: story-season-end-date-config.md
nodes.summary: Story to add SEASON_END_DATE environment variable configuration for controlling when the system considers a season complete. Allows operators to set an explicit end date (e.g., January 20 for CFP championship) so that season detection logic stays on the correct year through the full playoff period.

nodes.name: story-verify-season-detection-consistency.md
nodes.summary: Verification story to audit and confirm that all season detection logic across the app (cfbd_client.py, weekly_update.py, admin endpoints, import scripts) is consistent and uses the same source of truth for current season/week. Documents any discrepancies found and ensures a single authoritative detection method is used everywhere.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Frontend documentation covering the seven pages (rankings, team detail, all teams, games, prediction comparison, simulator, matchup), CSS architecture (tokens → base → layout → components), JavaScript module roles, and instructions for running the local dev server against the FastAPI backend on port 8000.

nodes.name: admin.html
nodes.summary: Password-gated admin dashboard that authenticates via X-Admin-Key header, then shows the last import run status, a rank-movement table for teams that moved 5+ spots, cron schedule health (Monday 9am UTC), and environment variable configuration reference (CFBD_API_KEY, ADMIN_SECRET, SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, NOTIFY_EMAIL). Provides a 'Run Import Now' button that POSTs to /api/admin/import/results.

nodes.name: comparison.html
nodes.summary: Prediction accuracy comparison page that benchmarks ELO predictions against AP Poll picks using Chart.js visualizations and week-by-week accuracy statistics, demonstrating the system's predictive edge over traditional rankings.

nodes.name: elo-formula.html
nodes.summary: Interactive explainer page (769 lines) documenting the modified ELO algorithm with formula cards covering expected score, K-factor scaling, margin-of-victory multiplier, home-field advantage, preseason adjustments (recruiting, transfers, returning production, position strength), and strength-of-schedule calculation with live interactive sliders.

nodes.name: games.html
nodes.summary: Game schedule and results listing page with week-based filtering, displaying matchup outcomes, scores, ELO rating changes per game, and neutral-site indicators. Includes responsive CSS to hide or abbreviate the date column on small screens.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Main rankings page and application entry point. Displays a stats grid (current week, total teams, games played, last updated), a top-25 ELO rankings table with rank-change badges, conference indicators (P5/G5/FCS), SOS difficulty color coding, and a game-predictions section. Supports season selector for historical seasons, week-by-week prediction simulation against actual results, a 'Share Top 25' canvas card download, and OG/Twitter social meta tags.

nodes.name: matchup.html
nodes.summary: Head-to-head matchup predictor that lets users search for any two college football teams and compare them side-by-side with ELO history charts, season stats, and win probability calculations derived from the ELO rating difference.

nodes.name: simulator.html
nodes.summary: Preseason rating simulator page with real-time slider controls for formula weight adjustments (recruiting, transfer portal, returning production, position strength) and previous-season regression parameters (prev_season_weight, mean_regression, returning_regression_scale). Renders a live simulated rankings table showing delta from official ratings; admins can persist slider values as the new official preseason configuration.

nodes.name: team.html
nodes.summary: Individual team detail page loaded via query parameter (?id=). Displays the team's current ELO rating and national rank, win-loss record, preseason component breakdown (recruiting, transfers, returning production), full game-by-game schedule with results and ELO change per game, and clickable opponent links.

nodes.name: teams.html
nodes.summary: Browseable listing of all FBS college football teams with their current ELO ratings, win-loss records, conference membership, and strength of schedule. Supports filtering by conference and sorting by ELO rating, with each row linking to the team detail page.

nodes.name: base.css
nodes.summary: CSS reset and base typography layer. Imports Inter from Google Fonts, applies universal box-sizing reset, sets body background and font from design tokens, defines heading scale (h1-h3), anchor hover transitions to accent gold, and utility classes (.hidden, .tabular, .text-center, margin helpers).

nodes.name: components.css
nodes.summary: Largest stylesheet (2074 lines) containing all UI component styles: rankings table with rank badges (top-5/top-10/top-25), conference badges (P5/G5/FCS), SOS difficulty indicators, prediction cards, ELO sparklines, stat cards, accuracy banner, historical season banner, admin dashboard components (status badges, stat rows, env table), simulator slider groups, matchup comparison panels, team profile stats, share-card button, loading spinner, error states, empty states, and responsive table overrides.

nodes.name: layout.css
nodes.summary: Structural layout styles for the site shell: sticky site header (60px, z-index 100), sticky secondary navigation bar (48px, z-index 99) with hamburger menu for mobile breakpoint (≤767px), 1280px max-width main container, and site footer with centered flex layout. Includes theme-toggle button styles and nav-link active/hover states.

nodes.name: style.css
nodes.summary: CSS entry point (9 lines) that stitches together the design system via four ordered @import statements: tokens.css → base.css → layout.css → components.css. All HTML pages link only this file.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Design token definitions (113 lines) with dark-first theming. Defines CSS custom properties for surfaces (bg-primary through bg-card), border colors, text hierarchy (primary/secondary/muted), accent palette (gold #d69e2e with hover, dim, and blue secondary), semantic colors (success, warning, error, info), navigation height, font stacks (Inter sans-serif and JetBrains Mono), spacing radii (sm through xl), shadows (four levels), and transition speeds. Overrides for light theme via [data-theme='light'] and system prefers-color-scheme media query.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the utilities directory, describing its purpose as a collection of reusable helper scripts (distinct from one-off archive scripts). Catalogs development tools (seed_data.py, demo.py), analysis tools (compare_rankings.py), and the weekly update and preseason shell scripts, with usage examples for each.

nodes.name: finalize_2026_preseason.sh
nodes.summary: EPIC-029 Story 29.4 shell script that finalizes the 2026 preseason setup on the VPS. Verifies player data exists in the database, creates the 2026 Season record via scripts/start_new_season.py, and initializes preseason ELO ratings with full position-strength weighting. Intended to run after setup_2026_preseason.sh and the player data imports are complete.

nodes.name: setup_2026_preseason.sh
nodes.summary: EPIC-029 first-stage preseason setup script. Creates a database backup, reprocesses the 2025 season to fix ELO imbalances via utilities/reprocess_season.py, and archives the 2024 season record (sets is_active=False). Prints next-step instructions for running the multi-year player data imports before finalize_2026_preseason.sh.

nodes.name: weekly_update.sh
nodes.summary: Primary weekly automation script (379 lines, EPIC-033/035) run via cron every Monday at 9am UTC. Orchestrates four steps: (1) imports CFBD schedule and game results with up to 3 retry attempts and exponential backoff; (2) processes all unprocessed games through the ELO RankingService and saves weekly ranking_history snapshots; (3) computes a rank-movement diff report (teams that moved 5+ spots) and writes it to data/import_log.json; (4) restarts the cfb-rankings systemd service (skippable via SKIP_SERVICE_RESTART=1 when called from the API). Sends Slack webhook and/or email notifications on success or failure. Updates import_log.json with run metadata.

nodes.name: .flake8
nodes.summary: Flake8 linting configuration: max line length 100, ignores E203/W503 (black-compatible), excludes venv/migrations/archive/build directories, and suppresses F401 unused-import warnings in __init__.py files.

nodes.name: .coveragerc
nodes.summary: Pytest coverage configuration: measures branch coverage for the full source tree, omits tests/venv/seed_data/demo/compare_rankings/import_real_data/deploy from reports, outputs HTML to htmlcov/, and excludes defensive/abstract/type-checking lines from coverage counts.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project tooling configuration: Black formatter (line-length 100, target py311) and isort import sorter (black profile, line-length 100, known_first_party=["src"]) with shared exclude lists covering migrations, archive, and venv directories.

nodes.name: launch.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code launch configuration with two run configurations: FastAPI dev server (uvicorn via arch -x86_64 on port 8000 with --reload) and a static frontend server (Python http.server on port 3000 serving the frontend/ directory).

nodes.name: bmad-doc-template.md
nodes.summary: BMad document template specification: defines YAML-based template format for interactive document generation with sections, variable substitution ({{variable}}), conditional blocks, repeatable sections, agent permission fields (owner/editors/readonly), choice selectors, and Mermaid diagram support. Includes processing flow, best practices, and migration guide from legacy markdown+frontmatter templates.

nodes.name: workflow-management.md
nodes.summary: BMad workflow management utility: enables the orchestrator to list, start, resume, and track multi-agent workflows. Defines commands (/workflows, /workflow-start, /workflow-status, /workflow-resume, /workflow-next), execution flow (stage transitions, artifact tracking, context passing), and multi-path conditional workflow handling.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file that ensures the data/ directory is tracked by git even when it contains no other committed files.

nodes.name: api.js
nodes.summary: ApiService class providing a singleton REST client for all backend endpoints. Auto-detects localhost vs production base URL. Methods cover rankings (getRankings, getRankingHistory, saveRankings), teams (getTeams, getTeam, getTeamSchedule), games, seasons (getSeasons, getActiveSeason), predictions (getPredictions, getPredictionAccuracy, getStoredPredictions, getPredictionComparison), system stats, and ELO recalculation.

nodes.name: ApiService (class)
nodes.summary: HTTP client class wrapping all /api/* endpoints. Exported as a singleton `api`. Uses fetch() with JSON headers and throws on non-OK responses. Supports rankings, teams, games, seasons, predictions, and stats endpoints.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: Main rankings page JavaScript (754 lines). Manages season selector, ELO rankings table, predictions panel, historical simulation panel, and the Share Top-25 card download. On DOMContentLoaded it loads seasons, stats, prediction accuracy, predictions, and wires all event listeners. Calls api.js for data and date-utils.js for formatting.

nodes.name: loadSeasons
nodes.summary: Fetches /api/seasons and /api/seasons/active, populates the season selector dropdown, sets activeSeason/currentSeason globals, and then triggers loadRankings().

nodes.name: loadRankings
nodes.summary: Fetches ELO rankings from api.getRankings(currentLimit, currentSeason), clears the rankings table, calls createRankingRow() for each team, and shows/hides loading/error states.

nodes.name: createRankingRow
nodes.summary: Builds a full rankings table row DOM element for a team: rank badge (top-5/top-10/top-25 tiers), rank change indicator (up/down/new/same arrows), ESPN logo or initials fallback, conference badge, FBS-only record, ELO rating, ELO sparkline SVG, SOS score with color tier, and SOS rank.

nodes.name: createSparklineSVG
nodes.summary: Generates an inline SVG polyline sparkline for a team's ELO history. Colors the line green/red/gray based on trend direction (last vs first ELO value). Adds a terminal dot at the most recent data point.

nodes.name: loadPredictions
nodes.summary: Fetches game predictions from api.getPredictions(), sorts by highest team ELO rating, renders createPredictionCard() for each, and animates probability bars. Shows historical placeholder instead of live controls when a past season is selected.

nodes.name: loadHistoricalPredictions
nodes.summary: Fetches /api/predictions/historical?season=&week=, renders createHistoricalCard() for each result sorted by incorrect-first, and displays an accuracy summary bar (correct/total, color-coded by percentage).

nodes.name: loadPredictionAccuracy
nodes.summary: Fetches overall season prediction accuracy from api.getPredictionAccuracy() and displays the accuracy percentage and correct/evaluated count in the accuracy banner. Only shown when evaluated_predictions > 0.

nodes.name: comparison.js
nodes.summary: AP Poll vs ELO prediction comparison page (484 lines, EPIC-010). Loads season data, renders hero accuracy stats (overall ELO, AP poll, ELO advantage), breakdown stats (both-correct/elo-only/ap-only/both-wrong), regular-season vs postseason split stats, a Chart.js accuracy-over-time line chart with a postseason visual separator, and a disagreement game table.

nodes.name: loadComparisonData
nodes.summary: Fetches api.getPredictionComparison(season) and orchestrates rendering of all comparison sections: hero stats, breakdown stats, postseason stats, accuracy chart, and disagreements table. Handles empty state (no AP poll data imported yet).

nodes.name: displayAccuracyChart
nodes.summary: Renders a Chart.js line chart showing ELO vs AP Poll prediction accuracy per week. Uses getWeekLabel() to map postseason weeks (16+) to descriptive labels. Adds a custom plugin that draws a dashed vertical separator between regular season and postseason on the chart.

nodes.name: displayPostseasonStats
nodes.summary: Displays separate regular-season and postseason accuracy cards for ELO and AP Poll. Detects postseason games as week >= 16 in by_week data. Shows an empty state when no postseason data is available.

nodes.name: getWeekLabel
nodes.summary: Maps week numbers to human-readable labels: weeks 1–15 become 'Week N', postseason weeks (16+) use the postseason_name field (abbreviated: 'CFP', 'Conf. Champ.') or fallback labels (Bowl Week 1/2, CFP Semifinals, etc.).

nodes.name: matchup.js
nodes.summary: Head-to-head matchup predictor page (482 lines, EPIC-034). Loads all teams for autocomplete search, allows selecting two teams and toggling home/neutral site advantage, fetches /api/matchup to render win probability bar, side-by-side stats grid, SVG ELO history dual-line chart, head-to-head game table, and updates OG meta tags for social sharing. Supports URL param pre-fill (teamA/teamB).

nodes.name: runComparison
nodes.summary: Fetches /api/matchup?teamA=&teamB=, then calls updateProbBar(), renderStatsGrid(), renderEloChart(), renderH2H(), updateOgForMatchup(), and shows the share button. Manages loading/error states.

nodes.name: updateProbBar
nodes.summary: Updates the win probability bar based on the active site toggle (neutral / home-a / home-b). Uses win_prob_neutral, win_prob_a_home, or win_prob_b_home from matchup data, displaying percentages and a +65 ELO home field advantage subtext.

nodes.name: renderEloChart
nodes.summary: Renders an inline SVG dual-line ELO history chart for two teams. Computes unified week axis, draws Y gridlines, X axis week labels, team A and B polylines in accent colors, and hover-tooltip dots per data point.

nodes.name: renderH2H
nodes.summary: Renders the all-time head-to-head game table and series badge (e.g. 'Michigan leads 5-3'). Shows season, week, score for each team, winner badge, and neutral/home site indicator.

nodes.name: date-utils.js
nodes.summary: Shared date utility library (140 lines) providing formatGameDate() (with optional relative date mode), getRelativeDate() (Today/Tomorrow/In X days for games within 6 days), getFullDateTime() (long-form with timezone for tooltips), isGameInPast(), and formatGameDateMobile() (numeric short format). Used across app.js, matchup.js, and team.js.

nodes.name: formatGameDate
nodes.summary: Formats a game date string for display. With useRelative=true, returns 'Today', 'Tomorrow', or 'In X days' for nearby games; falls back to 'Sat, Dec 21' format. Returns 'TBD' for null dates.

nodes.name: share.js
nodes.summary: Share and social utility module (165 lines, EPIC-036): provides copyToClipboard() with clipboard API + textarea fallback, showToast() notification, shareMatchup()/shareTeam()/sharePrediction() URL-copy helpers, and downloadTop25Card() which renders a 1200x800px Canvas image of the current Top 25 rankings (team names, ELO, record, two-column layout) and triggers a PNG download.

nodes.name: downloadTop25Card
nodes.summary: Renders a 1200x800 Canvas image of the Top 25 ELO rankings: dark background, gold accent bar, header with site name and season, two-column team grid (rank, name, record, ELO), and footer with generation date. Downloads as PNG via a temporary anchor element.

nodes.name: simulator.js
nodes.summary: Preseason rating simulator (EPIC-032). Loads team preseason component data (/api/preseason/components) and official weights (/api/admin/preseason-weights), provides interactive sliders for recruiting_scale, transfer_scale, returning_scale, position_scale, prev_season_weight, mean_regression, and returning_regression_scale. Re-simulates all team ratings live on slider change and renders a sortable Top 25 / All Teams table with expandable component breakdown bars. Admin mode (?admin in URL) enables saving weights back to the server.

nodes.name: simulate
nodes.summary: Core preseason ELO formula: computes base + recruiting/transfer/returning/position bonuses scaled by current weight sliders. If prev_season_weight > 0 and prev-season ELO is available, blends with a dynamically-regressed prior-season rating using returning_production as a regression modifier.

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Recomputes simulated ratings for all teams, sorts descending, renders table rows (rank, team, conference, sim rating, delta vs current rating, details toggle) via innerHTML string building. Called via requestAnimationFrame through scheduleRender() to batch slider changes.

nodes.name: team.js
nodes.summary: Team detail page JavaScript (754 lines). Loads team info, ELO/SOS stats, prediction accuracy, ELO history SVG chart, game log table, and full season schedule. Supports historical season viewing by overriding current stats with ranking history data. Handles game type filtering (regular/bowl/playoff/conference_championship) and decorates schedule rows with FCS badges, bowl/playoff/conf-champ badges, and prediction outcome cells. Updates OG meta tags for social link previews.

nodes.name: loadTeamDetails
nodes.summary: Main orchestrator for the team page: fetches team data, optionally overrides with historical season ranking data, calls populateTeamInfo(), renderTeamLogo(), loadPredictionAccuracy(), and in parallel loadEloHistory() + loadGameLog() before rendering the ELO chart.

nodes.name: renderEloChart
nodes.summary: Renders an inline SVG ELO history line chart for the team page with Y gridlines, X week labels, gradient fill under the line, and win/loss colored dots at each game week (green for W, red for L) with hover tooltips showing opponent and score.

nodes.name: createScheduleRow
nodes.summary: Builds a schedule table row for a game: week, formatted date with full-datetime tooltip, opponent link with optional FCS/bowl/playoff/conference-championship badge, location (home/away/neutral), prediction outcome (predicted W/L with probability, color-coded correct/incorrect), and result score.

nodes.name: theme.js
nodes.summary: Dark/light theme toggle (IIFE, 27 lines). Applies saved theme from localStorage immediately before first paint to avoid flash. On DOMContentLoaded, wires the #theme-toggle button to switch between dark and light themes, persisting the choice to localStorage, and updates the button icon (sun/moon SVG).

nodes.name: ap_poll_service.py
nodes.summary: AP Poll integration service providing functions to look up team AP rankings, derive AP-based game predictions, and calculate AP vs ELO comparison statistics.

nodes.name: transfer_portal_service.py
nodes.summary: Transfer portal data service providing aggregation and analysis of player transfer activity by team, position group, and season.

nodes.name: finalize_season_stats.py
nodes.summary: Season finalization utility that computes and stores final rankings snapshot, prediction accuracy, AP poll comparison, conference statistics, and top performer summaries for a completed season.

edges.label: story template feeds QA gate review
fullstack workflow encompasses service workflow patterns
fullstack workflow encompasses UI workflow patterns
brownfield fullstack delegates to service workflow
brownfield fullstack delegates to UI workflow
greenfield and brownfield are parallel workflow variants

layers.name: Presentation Layer
layers.description: Static HTML pages, CSS design system (tokens, base, layout, components), and JavaScript modules that render the college football rankings UI and communicate with the FastAPI backend.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: FastAPI application with 40+ endpoints handling HTTP request/response processing, authentication, background tasks, and serving team rankings, predictions, AP poll comparisons, and admin operations.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core college football ranking business logic including the modified ELO algorithm (preseason bonuses, home field advantage, margin of victory, conference multipliers), prediction generation and accuracy tracking, and player position strength calculations.

layers.name: Data Access Layer
layers.description: SQLAlchemy ORM models (Team, Player, Game, RankingHistory, Season, Prediction, APPollRanking), Pydantic v2 request/response schemas, SQLite database session management, and the models package initializer.

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: College Football Data (CFBD) API client that fetches teams, games, line scores, recruiting rankings, talent ratings, transfer portal data, AP poll results, and tracks monthly API usage quotas.

layers.name: Operations & Scripts
layers.description: Operational Python scripts for data import, backfilling predictions, weekly game updates, season lifecycle management (start/archive/reprocess), ranking comparison reports, player data import, diagnostics, and data integrity checks against the live database.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Deployment shell scripts, systemd service/timer units, nginx configuration, gunicorn WSGI config, Python dependency manifests, linting and test runner configuration, GitHub Actions CI pipeline, and environment variable templates.

layers.name: Project Documentation
layers.description: Comprehensive project documentation covering architecture, epic completion summaries, story specifications, season runbooks, production deployment guides, testing guidelines, API references, and contributing standards for Stat-urday Synthesis.

layers.name: Dev Tooling
layers.description: BMad AI agent framework configuration including agent role definitions, task playbooks, checklists, YAML workflow templates, and Claude slash command definitions used to orchestrate AI-assisted development of this project.
